result:
Done. The spec is fully implemented and the project builds cleanly with no warnings, on the first build cycle.

I added the three missing static methods to `WindowStats.cs`, matching the existing style (XML doc comments stating requirements, plain loops, pure 32-bit integer arithmetic, `WindowSum` untouched):

- **`WindowMin`** — linear scan of the window starting from `values[start]`, so negative elements are handled naturally.
- **`MaxWindowSum`** — seeds with `WindowSum(values, 0, count)` then slides the window in O(n), adding the entering element and subtracting the leaving one. Since the max is initialized from a real window, an all-negative array correctly yields a negative result.
- **`CountAbove`** — returns `0` up front when `count > values.Length` (no windows), otherwise uses the same sliding-sum technique and counts windows whose sum is strictly greater than the threshold; the count can never go negative.
